response.justification: Candidate has 14+ years of broad software engineering experience but the role requires a seasoned C++ developer for industrial automation. C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a bachelor thesis; the rest of the career is dominated by DLang, TypeScript, Node.js, and web/full-stack work. No evidence of IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, industrial protocols, or message-broker-based large-scale C++ systems. Some adjacent strengths (multi-language adaptability, low-latency systems in DLang, React/Node.js) exist but do not offset the core C++ and industrial-cybersecurity gap.
strengths: 14+ years engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices at Sociomantic)
Familiarity with React, Node.js, and DevOps/CI tooling (advantageous skills)
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience, dated to 2014–2015
No demonstrated experience with IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity
No exposure to industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT) or automation domain

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IEC 62443 background
Strong general engineering breadth and tenure
